title: Use-After-Free, Double Free, and Stale Aliases · Memory Retrieval
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the generation-checked handle repair contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for generation-checked handle repair after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace allocation, publication, last valid use, deallocation, stale use, and address reuse to diagnose use-after-free.
Prevent double free by assigning exactly one release responsibility per live ownership state.
Invalidate pointers to subobjects and one-past positions when the containing allocation lifetime ends.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
